title: Oxyanthus tubiflorus
intro: Oxyanthus tubiflorus, commonly known as the Forest Yellow Wood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Oxyanthus tubiflorus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Oxyanthus tubiflorus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family is commonly known as the coffee or madder family, consisting of approximately 660 genera and 13,000 species. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Oxyanthus tubiflorus is also classified as a part of the order Gentianales. Within the family Rubiaceae, Oxyanthus tubiflorus is a part of the tribe Psychotrieae. Oxyanthus tubiflorus is a genus of flowering plants native to tropical Africa.
